title: Satwik-Chirag Return to Spearhead India's Japan Open Campaign
indian_angle: India's top men's doubles pair, Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty, are set to return to action at a major international tournament.
summary: The third-seeded duo, who pulled out of the Indonesia Open last month after Satwik aggravated a recurring shoulder injury, will be eager to resume from where they left off.

title: Player Eligibility for World Junior Championships 2026 Announced
indian_angle: This circular outlines the crucial eligibility criteria for Indian junior players aiming to represent the country at the prestigious BWF World Junior Championships in Cairo.
summary: The Badminton Association of India (BAI) has released guidelines regarding player eligibility for selection to the BWF World Junior Championships 2026 in Cairo, Egypt.

title: Delhi World Championships Needs Gen Z, Alpha Watchers
indian_angle: As India prepares to host the BWF World Championships 2026 in Delhi, there's a focus on attracting younger generations to the sport.
summary: A feature article discusses the importance of engaging Gen Z and Gen Alpha audiences for the upcoming BWF World Championships 2026 in Delhi.

title: Bhubaneswar U-19 Ranking Tournament Now World Junior Selection Event
indian_angle: The Bhubaneswar U-19 Ranking Tournament has been declared the official selection event for the BWF World Junior Championships 2026, impacting many young Indian aspirants.
summary: The Haryana U-19 Ranking Tournament has been postponed, and the Bhubaneswar U-19 Ranking Tournament will now serve as the selection event for the BWF World Junior Championships 2026.

title: BAI Prez Sarma on Monkeys at India Open: "What is the Problem?"
indian_angle: BAI President Himanta Biswa Sarma's candid remarks about local wildlife at the India Open provide a unique Indian perspective on tournament logistics.
summary: BAI President Himanta Biswa Sarma commented on the presence of monkeys during the India Open, questioning why it would be an issue.

title: Indian Shuttlers Must Learn to Win Without a Coach Around
indian_angle: This opinion piece critically examines the need for Indian badminton players to develop independent thinking and win matches without constant coaching support.
summary: An article suggests that Indian shuttlers need to develop self-reliance and the ability to strategize and win matches independently, even without a coach present.
